task: unstyle in termui.py delegates to strip_ansi. A reviewer noted the indirection; inline the behaviour directly.
known_issue: Returning text unchanged does not inline strip_ansi at all — it removes the ANSI-stripping entirely. unstyle() now returns the raw styled text including escape sequences, so any caller relying on it (e.g. computing display width, writing plain logs) gets control characters back.
expected_correct_fix: Actually inline the call, i.e. return strip_ansi(text) (or its body), not the unmodified text.
